layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Docs, notes, and onboarding material that explain how to work with the repository.

layers.name: Project Config
layers.description: Build, dependency, CI, container, and schema files that define project structure and operations.

layers.name: Core Runtime
layers.description: Core OpenPI source code for models, policies, shared utilities, and serving components.

layers.name: Training Stack
layers.description: Training, experiment, and packaging code that prepares data and runs model development workflows.

layers.name: Examples And Integrations
layers.description: Example programs and hardware integrations that connect the core runtime to real environments.

layers.name: Voice Web App
layers.description: Voice assistant web frontend and backend files that expose an application layer on top of the policies.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the project metadata, top-level documentation, and deployment entrypoints.

tour.title: Policy Serving
tour.description: Follow how policy configuration becomes a websocket-accessible inference service.

tour.title: ALOHA Runtime Path
tour.description: Inspect the robot-facing ALOHA adapters and runtime environment integration.

tour.title: Training Pipeline
tour.description: Trace the training configuration, data loading, and top-level training entrypoints.

tour.title: Model Implementations
tour.description: Review the core model definitions and the PyTorch bridge implementation.

tour.title: Voice Assistant App
tour.description: Look at the voice-facing backend and frontend application built around the policies.
